Both fixes landed and verified.

**Summary**

1. **Grep body highlighting** (`crates/jfc-ui/src/message_view/formatters.rs:48-148`)
   - `pathless_target` is now hoisted out so single-file greps can highlight even when each line omits the path.
   - The match body now passes through `markdown::highlight_code_raw` when `lang_from_path` resolves an extension (from either `path` or `pathless_target`), with a flat `body_color` fallback when syntect returns no rows.

2. **Diff hunk resilience** — both renderers had the same brittle `hl.len() == hunk.len()` shunt that dropped *all* color on a single-line drift:
   - `formatters.rs::produce_git_diff_output_lines` flush closure (~L363) now retries per-line via `highlight_code_raw` when the hunk-level match misses, before falling back to flat text.
   - `outputs.rs::produce_diff_view_lines` (~L121) got the same per-line retry path, plus a hoisted `push_hl_spans` helper to keep the bg-tint / DIM logic in one place.

Verified with `cargo check -p jfc-ui` (clean) and `cargo test -p jfc-ui --lib` (25/25 pass).

The pre-existing `let _ =` warnings flagged in the evaluator output are in unrelated files (`jfc-graph`, and lines 258-259 / 1064 of formatters.rs that I never touched) — left alone per scope.
